sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge-graph systems organize entities and relations for semantic search, integration, multi-hop retrieval, probabilistic inference, and grounded generation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Knowledge graph, RAG, probabilistic graphical model, GNN, and transformer references collectively support entities/relations, retrieval, inference, and grounded generation.

sentence_reviews.text: They preserve explicit structure and lineage that can be difficult to recover from embeddings or parameters alone.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Graph and probabilistic-model references support explicit relational structure; the contrast with embeddings or parameters is defensible synthesis.

edge_reviews.notes: Knowledge graph systems directly depend on graph/ontology/schema representations.
Semantic search, multi-hop retrieval, and grounded generation support Graph RAG as common.
Probabilistic inference over relations supports Bayesian/factor graph dependency.
Graph transformers are plausible for relational reasoning, but packet sources only indirectly support the exact dependency.
Lineage and grounded generation support provenance/citation tracking.
Rule systems are a plausible optional layer over explicit knowledge graphs.
Probabilistic programming can express relational uncertainty, but the cited packet sources do not directly establish it as a dependency.
